policy_statement: Mass provenance must be classified as metadata_table curated_registry instrument_header_provisional or filename_derived_audited_only
Filename-derived mass is forbidden unless explicit audited waiver exists
Mass assignment must prefer sample_id or curated file_id_to_sample_id mapping
run_id may scope audit context but cannot be sole key for mass identity assignment
Row-level sample_mass_g is propagated metadata and not independent per-row measurement
Mass provenance unit must be explicitly grams
Mass provenance source class must be explicitly recorded
No implicit default mass values are allowed
Mass must be consistent across files mapped to the same sample under declared tolerance
Replicated row-level mass values must match mapped sample/file mass
If mass missing or rejected then M_norm_emu_per_g is NaN or blocked
If mass missing or rejected then chi_mass_emu_per_g_per_Oe is NaN or blocked
Raw M_emu or M_over_H cannot be relabeled as normalized quantities when mass missing
Allowed normalized outputs are descriptive proxies only and not absolute material constants
chi_mass usage requires nonzero-field guard in addition to mass provenance gates
Normalization-dependent observables remain BLOCKED_PENDING_PROVENANCE until mass policy gates pass
